# Sift

> URL → clean Markdown + structured JSON for LLMs and RAG pipelines.

Sift is an API service that extracts clean, token-efficient Markdown and structured JSON from any URL. It is designed for AI applications — RAG pipelines, agents, and chatbots that need to stay in sync with external web content.

## What makes it different

Most "URL to Markdown" tools are one-shot. Sift's primary feature is scheduled change detection: register a URL, and Sift re-crawls it on a schedule, computes a semantic diff section-by-section, and fires a webhook only when content meaningfully changed. No noise from cosmetic updates.

The extraction engine is tuned for documentation sites: Docusaurus, Sphinx/ReadTheDocs, ReadMe, Mintlify, GitBook, and MkDocs are all handled with framework-specific content targeting, not just generic Readability.

## Output formats

Every extraction returns:
- `markdown` — clean, normalized Markdown (nav, footers, ads, cookie banners stripped)
- `json.sections` — heading tree with content per section
- `json.codeBlocks` — code blocks with language tags preserved
- `json.tables` — tables as typed arrays (headers + rows)
- `content_hash` — SHA-256 of the normalized Markdown (stable across repeated fetches)
- `token_count` — cl100k-basis token estimate

## API

Base URL: https://sift.dev/api/v1
Authentication: `Authorization: Bearer sk_live_...`

### POST /api/v1/extract
Extract a URL to Markdown + JSON.

Request body:
- `url` (string, required) — the URL to extract
- `niche` ("docs" | "auto", optional) — "docs" enables doc-framework-specific tuning
- `render_mode` ("fetch" | "playwright" | "auto", optional) — "auto" falls back to headless browser for JS-rendered pages

Response includes: url, title, description, markdown, json (sections/codeBlocks/tables), content_hash, token_count, render_mode, meta, _quota

### GET /api/v1/usage
Returns current period quota: used, limit, remaining, reset_at.

### POST /api/v1/sources
Register a URL for scheduled monitoring.

Request body:
- `url` (string, required)
- `niche` ("docs" | "auto", optional)
- `schedule_interval` (integer seconds | null) — null = one-time only

### GET /api/v1/sources/:id/diffs
Returns the change history for a monitored source. Each diff has: change_summary, added_sections, removed_sections, changed_sections, changed_at.

## Webhook payload (source.changed event)

```json
{
  "event": "source.changed",
  "source_id": "...",
  "url": "https://...",
  "change_summary": "Updated: \"Installation\". Added: \"Advanced\"",
  "diff": {
    "added": ["Advanced"],
    "removed": [],
    "changed": ["Installation"]
  },
  "timestamp": "2024-03-15T10:30:00.000Z"
}
```

Webhook payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256. Verify using the `X-Sift-Signature` header.

## Tiers

- Free (no card): 100 extractions/month, 2 sources, daily schedule
- Pro ($19/month): 5,000 extractions, 50 sources, hourly schedule, webhooks
- Team ($99/month): 50,000 extractions, 500 sources, every 15 minutes

## Links

- Docs: https://sift.dev/docs
- Dashboard: https://sift.dev/dashboard
- Sign up: https://sift.dev/login
